category_title: Middle school students with IDD and low reading levels who independently navigate digital literacy environments using audio supports and age-relevant content
scenario_title: Managing disappointment when peer notifications are absent
minimal_context: The student is logging into the Udio platform at the beginning of the session to check for peer notifications.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please log into the Udio platform to begin our session.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Navigates to the notifications area as the first task) It’s fun. Because you get to, like, talk to them and stuff.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, let's see what is there.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Checks the screen) I don't have any new comments from my peers.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Alright, let's move on to the reading activity now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Shows evident disappointment that no notifications were present and refuses to close the empty notification screen)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please log into the Udio platform to begin our session.

STUDENT: (Navigates to the notifications area as the first task) It’s fun. Because you get to, like, talk to them and stuff.

TUTOR: Okay, let's see what is there.

STUDENT: (Checks the screen) I don't have any new comments from my peers.

TUTOR: Alright, let's move on to the reading activity now.

STUDENT: (Shows evident disappointment that no notifications were present and refuses to close the empty notification screen)

task.content: Checking notifications of whether they had received comments from peers

context.social_setting: Online discussions with peers and teachers

related_quotes: It’s fun. Because you get to, like, talk to them and stuff.
